title: Existence of intermediate discount factor where two monotone value trajectories coincide
background_latex:
Let \(K=\{1,\dots,k\}\) be a finite state space and let \(M\) be an irreducible stochastic matrix on \(K\) with unique invariant distribution \(\pi_M\in\Delta(K)\). Consider a Markovian persuasion game with state process \((X_n)_{n\ge 1}\) evolving as a Markov chain with transition matrix \(M\), where a sender privately observes \(X_n\) each period and sends a signal to a receiver. The receiver forms a posterior belief \(p_n\in\Delta(K)\) and then chooses an action \(b(p_n)\) (assumed to follow expected-utility maximization with tie-breaking in favor of the sender). The sender’s stage payoff depends on the state and the receiver action; equivalently define the induced payoff function \(u:\Delta(K)\to\mathbb{R}_+\) by
\[
 u(\xi)=\sum_{\ell\in K}\xi_\ell\,V(\ell,b(\xi)).
\]
For a discount factor \(\delta\in[0,1)\), let \(v_\delta(q)\) denote the sender’s optimal \(\delta\)-discounted value starting from prior \(q\in\Delta(K)\).

Define two functions of \(\delta\in[0,1)\) (for irreducible \(M\)):
\[
\Phi(\delta):=v_\delta(\pi_M),
\qquad
\Psi(\delta):=\sum_{\ell\in K}\pi_{M,\ell}\,v_\delta(e_\ell),
\]
where \(e_\ell\in\Delta(K)\) is the Dirac belief on state \(\ell\), and \(\pi_{M,\ell}\) is the \(\ell\)-th coordinate of \(\pi_M\). In the paper, \(\Phi\) is shown to be non-increasing in \(\delta\) (for stationary prior \(\pi_M\)), and \(\Psi\) is shown to be non-decreasing in \(\delta\) (as a particular instance of an increasing weighted-average trajectory). Also, concavity of \(v_\delta(\cdot)\) implies \(\Phi(\delta)\ge \Psi(\delta)\) for all \(\delta\in[0,1)\).
problem_definition_latex:
Determine whether there exists a Markovian persuasion game (i.e., some finite \(K\), irreducible transition matrix \(M\), receiver action set and payoff primitives inducing \(u\)) such that there exists \(\delta_0\in(0,1)\) with
\[
\Phi(\delta_0)=\Psi(\delta_0),
\]
where \(\Phi(\delta)=v_\delta(\pi_M)\) and \(\Psi(\delta)=\sum_{\ell\in K}\pi_{M,\ell} v_\delta(e_\ell)\).
(Equivalently: can equality between these two monotone trajectories occur at some strictly interior discount factor \(\delta_0\in(0,1)\), rather than only at \(\delta_0=0\) or in the limit \(\delta\to 1^-\)?)
latex:

Consider a Markovian persuasion game with state process \((X_n)_{n\ge 1}\) evolving as a Markov chain with transition matrix \(M\), where a sender privately observes \(X_n\) each period and sends a signal to a receiver. The sender’s stage payoff depends on the state and the receiver action; equivalently define the induced payoff function \(u:\Delta(K)\to\mathbb{R}_+\) by
\[

\]
For a discount factor \(\delta\in[0,1)\), let \(v_\delta(q)\) denote the sender’s optimal \(\delta\)-discounted value starting from prior \(q\in\Delta(K)\).

Define two functions of \(\delta\in[0,1)\) (for irreducible \(M\)):
\[
\Phi(\delta):=v_\delta(\pi_M),

Determine whether there exists a Markovian persuasion game (i.e., some finite \(K\), irreducible transition matrix \(M\), receiver action set and payoff primitives inducing \(u\)) such that there exists \(\delta_0\in(0,1)\) with
\[
\Phi(\delta_0)=\Psi(\delta_0),
\]
where \(\Phi(\delta)=v_\delta(\pi_M)\) and \(\Psi(\delta)=\sum_{\ell\in K}\pi_{M,\ell} v_\delta(e_\ell)\).
(Equivalently: can equality between these two monotone trajectories occur at some strictly interior discount factor \(\delta_0\in(0,1)\), rather than only at \(\delta_0=0\) or in the limit \(\delta\to 1^-\)?)

open_problem_quote: Nevertheless, the existence of an example for Case B in which \(0 < \delta_0 < 1\) (where \(\delta_0\) was described in Corollary 1) remains an open question.
subject_classification: dynamic Bayesian persuasion / repeated games with incomplete information
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The question asks for existence of a concrete example, which suggests automated search over small finite state spaces and discretized payoff functions could be attempted; however the space of payoff primitives and receiver best responses is large and constraints (e.g., tie-breaking, induced u, computing v_\delta) are nontrivial, limiting brute-force counterexample searches.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Candidate instances (small \(K\), matrices \(M\), and piecewise-linear \(u\)) can be encoded and evaluated by solving the associated dynamic program for \(v_\delta\) at sampled \(\delta\) values, giving an automatic fitness signal for whether \(\Phi(\delta)-\Psi(\delta)\) crosses zero in \((0,1)\). However exact verification and ensuring the instance corresponds to valid persuasion primitives may require additional modeling work.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.
